title: Resilient Growth and Inflation Dynamics in a Mixed Macro Backdrop
content: Across the period, macro data pointed to broader economic resilience despite slowing momentum in parts of the world. Inflation in many advanced economies remained stable or moderating (euro area near target, Japan still above BoJ goal, UK inflation expected to fall), encouraging supportive policy narratives. Growth forecasts for 2026 were modestly upward revised by agencies, reflecting solid consumption and labour markets in the US and strong emerging market performance. However, risks around slowing manufacturing and services in some regions persisted, and data irregularities from prior US reporting disruptions complicated interpretation. Mixed growth-inflation dynamics continued to shape equity, credit and commodity markets, reinforcing the theme that pricing power and yield curves will be sensitive to incoming data.

title: Geopolitical and Trade Policy Uncertainty Impacting Risk Sentiment
content: Geopolitical tensions and trade policy developments have been a persistent theme through this window, amplifying volatility in risk assets. Threats of new US tariffs in response to diplomatic disputes triggered sharp equity market sell-offs in January and repricing of risk premiums across global indices. The market reaction underscored the sensitivity of equities and safe-haven assets (gold and FX) to political shocks. Meanwhile, broader geopolitical developments such as Venezuelan turmoil and trade tensions contributed to cautious positioning, influencing FX dynamics and risk spreads. Trade and political risk remain key to assessing short-term market sentiment and cross-border capital flows.
